titan-glove.tooltip: Increases melee knockback. Increases the size of melee weapons

titanium-armor.name: Titanium armor
titanium-armor.tooltip: Will give the Titanium Barrier buff for 10 seconds when you hit an enemy.

titanium-mask.name: Titanium Mask
titanium-mask.tooltip: 9% increased melee damage and critical strike chance. 9% increased melee speed

titanium-helmet.name: Titanium Helmet
titanium-helmet.tooltip: 16% increased ranged damage. 7% increased ranged critical strike chance

titanium-headgear.name: Titanium Headgear

titanium-leggings.name: Titanium Leggings
titanium-leggings.tooltip: 3% increased damage and critical strike chance. 6% increased movement speed

titanium-crate.name: Titanium Crate
titanium-crate.tooltip: Can be caught while fishing in any biome (hardmode).

titanium-ore.name: Titanium Ore
titanium-ore.tooltip:
One of the two top-tier ores that can spawn from every 3rd destroyed {{Demon Altar}} or {{Crimson Altar}} in {{Hardmode}} (the other possible ore being {{Adamantite Ore}}). It's used to make {{Titanium Bar}}.

Requires at least {{Mythril Pickaxe}} or {{Orichalcum Pickaxe}}.

terra-toilet.name: Terra Toilet
terra-toilet.tooltip: Seriously? THIS is what you used the Broken Hero Sword for?

tomb-crawler-banner.name: Tomb Crawler Banner
tomb-crawler-banner.tooltip: Nearby players get a bonus against: Tomb Crawler

tome-of-infinite-wisdom.name: Tome of Infinite Wisdom
tome-of-infinite-wisdom.tooltip: Gotta wonder who stuck a tome of infinite wisdom on a stick.... Right click to cast a powerful tornado

toolbox.tooltip: Increases block placement & tool range by 1

topaz-robe.name: Topaz Robe
topaz-robe.tooltip: Increases maximum mana by 40. 7% reduced mana cost

torch-gods-favor.name: Torch God's Favor

toxic-sludge-banner.name: Toxic Sludge Banner
toxic-sludge-banner.tooltip: Nearby players get a bonus against: Toxic Sludge

tragic-umbrella.name: Tragic Umbrella
tragic-umbrella.tooltip: You will fall slower while holding this

tree-globe.name: Tree Globe
tree-globe.tooltip: Toss it to change how trees look!. 'Time for a change of scenery'

trident.name: Trident
trident.tooltip: Increases mobility in water when held. Hold Up to descend slower

tropical-smoothie.name: Tropical Smoothie
tropical-smoothie.tooltip: Minor improvements to all stats. 'Real smooth'

truffle-worm.name: Truffle Worm
truffle-worm.tooltip:
A rare {{Hardmode}} critter and boss-summoning item that spawns in the underground {{Glowing Mushroom biome}}.

It is used as bait to summon {{Duke Fishron}} in the {{Ocean}} biome.

Can be caught with {{Bug Net}}{{Lavaproof Bug Net}}{{Golden Bug Net}}.

tsunami-in-a-bottle.name: Tsunami in a Bottle
tsunami-in-a-bottle.tooltip: Allows the holder to double jump

tungsten-ore.name: Tungsten Ore
tungsten-ore.tooltip: An early game ore which spawns in the {{Underground}} and {{Cavern}} layers as well as on Floating Islands. It's used to make {{Tungsten Bar}}.

turtle-helmet.name: Turtle Helmet
turtle-helmet.tooltip: 6% increased melee damage. Enemies are more likely to target you

turtle-leggings.name: Turtle Leggings
turtle-leggings.tooltip: 4% increased melee critical strike chance. Enemies are more likely to target you

lazer-blazer.name: Lazer Blazer
lazer-blazer.tooltip: 'How bad could business possibly be?'. Concept by Dr.Zootsuit

pinstripe-pants.name: Pinstripe Pants

ultrabright-helmet.name: Ultrabright Helmet

umbrella.name: Umbrella

umbrella-slime-banner.tooltip: Nearby players get a bonus against: Umbrella Slime

closed-void-bag.tooltip: Summons the void vault. Functions as an extended inventory when open. May pick up overflowing items when open. Right click to open. 'This pocket dimension is out of this world!'

void-vault.name: Void Vault
void-vault.tooltip: Can be used to store your items. Stored items can only be accessed by you. Will contain items picked up by a void bag

volatile-gelatin.name: Volatile Gelatin
volatile-gelatin.tooltip: Releases volatile gelatin periodically that damages enemies

vortex-armor.name: Vortex armor
vortex-armor.tooltip: Double tap "Down" to toggle stealth, increasing ranged ability and reducing chance for enemy to target you but slowing movement.

vortex-helmet.name: Vortex Helmet
vortex-helmet.tooltip: 16% increased ranged damage. 7% increased ranged critical strike chance

vortex-breastplate.name: Vortex Breastplate

vortex-leggings.name: Vortex Leggings

vortex-beater.name: Vortex Beater
vortex-beater.tooltip: 66% chance to save ammo. 'The catastrophic mixture of pew pew and boom boom.'

vortexian-banner.name: Vortexian Banner

vulture-banner.name: Vulture Banner

wandering-yukata.name: Wandering Yukata

wandering-geta.name: Wandering Geta

waterleaf.name: Waterleaf

betsys-wings.tooltip: Allows flight and slow fall. Press Down to toggle hover. Press Up to deactivate hover

ghostars-infinity-eight.tooltip: 'Great for impersonating devs!'. Allows flight and slow fall. 'I am right here'

safemans-blanket-cape.tooltip: 'Great for impersonating devs!'. Allows flight and slow fall. 'Can you help me tie this on?'

foodbarbarians-tattered-dragon-wings.tooltip: 'Great for impersonating devs!'. Allows flight and slow fall. 'The holes cut down on weight.'

empress-wings.tooltip: Allows flight and slow fall. Hold Up to boost faster!

wither-beast-banner.name: Wither Beast Banner

5% increased magic damage.

The Wizard Hat is part of the Wizard set, which further consists of a {{Mystic Robe}} or any gem-augmented Robe. It provides a set bonus of 10% magic critical strike chance.

wolf-banner.name: Wolf Banner
wolf-banner.tooltip: Nearby players get a bonus against: Wolf

wooden-crate.name: Wooden Crate
wooden-crate.tooltip: Can be caught while fishing in any biome (pre-hardmode).

wraith-banner.name: Wraith Banner
wraith-banner.tooltip: Nearby players get a bonus against: Wraith

wyvern-banner.tooltip: Nearby players get a bonus against: Wyvern

yellow-horseshoe-balloon.tooltip: Allows the holder to double jump. Increases jump height and negates fall damage

yellow-slime-banner.name: Yellow Slime Banner
yellow-slime-banner.tooltip: Nearby players get a bonus against: Yellow Slime

yeti-banner.name: Yeti Banner
yeti-banner.tooltip: Nearby players get a bonus against: Yeti

yoraiz0rs-scowl.name: Yoraiz0r's Scowl
yoraiz0rs-scowl.tooltip: 'Great for impersonating devs!'. If you see this you should probably run away...

yoraiz0rs-spell.name: Yoraiz0r's Spell
yoraiz0rs-spell.tooltip: 'Great for impersonating devs!'. Allows flight and slow fall. Whatever this accessory does to you is not a bug!

yoyo-bag.name: Yoyo Bag
yoyo-bag.tooltip: Gives the user master yoyo skills

yoyo-glove.name: Yoyo Glove
yoyo-glove.tooltip: Allows the use of two yoyos at once

zombie-elf-banner.name: Zombie Elf Banner
zombie-elf-banner.tooltip: Nearby players get a bonus against: Zombie Elf

zombie-merman-banner.name: Zombie Merman Banner
zombie-merman-banner.tooltip: Nearby players get a bonus against: Zombie Merman

dryad.name: Dryad
dryad.tooltip: Spawn condition: {{@d Defeat}} {{Eye of Cthulhu}} or {{Eater of Worlds}} or {{Brain of Cthulhu}} or {{Skeletron}}

mechanic.name: Mechanic
mechanic.tooltip: Spawn condition: Talk to her after {{@d defeating}} {{Skeletron}} (She is found in the {{Dungeon}})

skeleton-merchant.name: Skeleton Merchant
skeleton-merchant.tooltip: NPC vendor that randomly spawns in the {{Cavern}} layer.

tavernkeep.name: Tavernkeep
tavernkeep.tooltip: Spawn condition: {{@d Defeat}} {{Eater of Worlds}} or {{Brain of Cthulhu}} then find and talk to the tavernkeep (he can spawn anywhere)

arms-dealer.name: Arms Dealer
arms-dealer.tooltip: Spawn condition: Have {{@n 1 }} gun or bullet in the inventory

traveling-merchant.name: Traveling Merchant
traveling-merchant.tooltip: A unique NPC vendor who randomly visits the player's town for one day. His inventory is randomized and different every visit.

golfer.name: Golfer
golfer.tooltip: Spawn condition: Talk to him (he is found in the {{Underground Desert}})

truffle.name: Truffle
truffle.tooltip: Spawn condition: House in a Surface {{Glowing Mushroom biome}}. Surface Glowing Mushroom biomes do not generate naturally and therefore must be manually created.

zoologist.name: Zoologist
zoologist.tooltip: Spawn condition: The Bestiary has been filled to at least {{@n 10%}} ({{@n 53}} entries).

party-girl.name: Party Girl
party-girl.tooltip: Spawn condition: {{@n 8}} other town NPCs are in the world

wizard.name: Wizard
wizard.tooltip: Spawn condition: {{@d Defeat}} {{Wall of Flesh}}, then find him in the {{Cavern}} layer or below.

princess.name: Princess
princess.tooltip: Spawn condition: All other town NPCs (not including {{Santa Claus}} or town pets) are present.

steampunker.name: Steampunker
steampunker.tooltip: Spawn condition: {{@d Defeat}} any of the {{mechanical bosses}}

dye-trader.name: Dye Trader
dye-trader.tooltip: Spawn condition: Have a dye, dye ingredient, or {{Strange Plant}} in your inventory; {{@n 4}} other town NPCs are present

demolitionist.name: Demolitionist
demolitionist.tooltip: Spawn condition: Have any explosive, {{Merchant}} is present

santa-claus.name: Santa Claus
santa-claus.tooltip: Spawn condition: {{@d Defeat}} {{Frost Legion}} (unlocks him on {{@n December 15 - December 31}}) {{@s *OR*}} complete the {{Frost Moon}} (unlocks him for a day)

stylist.name: Stylist
stylist.tooltip: Spawn condition: Talk to her (she is found in Spider Caves, entangled in cobweb)

goblin-tinkerer.name: Goblin Tinkerer
goblin-tinkerer.tooltip: Spawn condition: {{@d Defeat}} a {{Goblin Army}}, then talk to him (he is found in the {{Cavern}} layer)

painter.name: Painter
painter.tooltip: Spawn condition: {{@n 4}} other town NPCs are present

angler.name: Angler
angler.tooltip: Spawn condition: Talk to him (he is found on the sand or on the water surface of the Ocean biome)

small-star-cell-banner.name: Small Star Cell Banner
small-star-cell-banner.tooltip: Nearby players get a bonus against: Small Star Cell

betsy.name: Betsy
betsy.tooltip: A boss that appears during the final wave of the {{Old One's Army}} {{@n Tier 3}} event.

celestial-pillars.name: Celestial Pillars
celestial-pillars.tooltip:
Four late-Hardmode bosses that appear during the {{Lunar Events}} after the {{Lunatic Cultist}} has been defeated: {{solar pillar}}{{vortex pillar}}{{stardust pillar}}{{nebula pillar}}.

They appear at four locations equally spaced across the map, and are completely stationary.

dark-mage.name: Dark Mage
dark-mage.tooltip: A miniboss in the final wave of the {{Old One's Army}} {{@n Tier 1}} event.

deerclops.name: Deerclops
deerclops.tooltip: Summon: Use the {{Deer Thing}} at any time while in the {{Snow Biome}}

duke-fishron.name: Duke Fishron
duke-fishron.tooltip: Summon: Use {{Truffle Worm}} as bait for fishing in the {{Ocean}} Biome

everscream.name: Everscream
everscream.tooltip: A miniboss that spawns during the {{Frost Moon}} event.

eye-of-cthulhu.name: Eye of Cthulhu
eye-of-cthulhu.tooltip: Summon: Use {{Suspicious Looking Eye}} at night.

goblin-summoner-banner.name: Goblin Summoner Banner
goblin-summoner-banner.tooltip: Nearby players get a bonus against: Goblin Summoner

golem.name: Golem
golem.tooltip: Summon: Use {{Lihzahrd Power Cell}} at {{Lihzahrd Altar}} in the {{Jungle Temple}} after defeating {{Plantera}}

guide.name: Guide
guide.tooltip: Spawn condition: The guide spawns near the player when the world is created.

honey-comb.name: Honey Comb
honey-comb.tooltip: Releases bees and douses the user in honey when damaged

ice-queen.name: Ice Queen
ice-queen.tooltip: The third and final miniboss that spawns during the {{Frost Moon}} event.

king-slime.name: King Slime
king-slime.tooltip: Summon: Use {{Slime Crown}} {{@s *OR*}} {{@d kill}} {{@n 150}} slimes during {{Slime Rain}} ({{@n 75}} if he has been defeated before)

kites.name: Kites
kites.tooltip: Kites can be flown on windy days. Reel it in with . ⚷.  Open / Activate

lunatic-cultist.name: Lunatic Cultist
lunatic-cultist.tooltip: Summon: {{@d Kill}} all {{@n 4}} cultists at the {{Dungeon}} entrance (after {{Golem}} is {{@d defeated}})

martian-probe.name: Martian Probe
martian-probe.tooltip:
A rare {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Golem}} flying enemy that spawns above the surface in the outer two-thirds of the world, usually in the {{Space}} layer.

It starts with a green light but turns red upon detecting the player, and tries to escape. If it does, it triggers the {{Martian Madness}} event.

martian-saucer.name: Martian Saucer
martian-saucer.tooltip: A large flying miniboss that spawns during the {{Martian Madness}} event.

mimics.name: Mimics
mimics.tooltip: Mimics are rare {{Hardmode}} {{enemies}} disguised as {{chests}}, appearing as the appropriate chest type for the environment.

moon-lord.name: Moon Lord
moon-lord.tooltip: Summon: {{@d Defeat}} all {{@n 4}} {{Celestial Pillars}} from the {{Lunar Events}} {{@s *OR*}} Use {{Celestial Sigil}}

mourning-wood.name: Mourning Wood
mourning-wood.tooltip: A miniboss that spawns during the {{Pumpkin Moon}}.

nurse.name: Nurse
nurse.tooltip: Spawn condition: 100+ max health, {{Merchant}} is present

ogre.name: Ogre
ogre.tooltip: A miniboss in the final wave of the {{Old One's Army}} {{@n Tier 2}} event.

old-man.name: Old Man
old-man.tooltip: NPC found at the Dungeon's entrance. When spoken to at night, the Old Man will give the player the option to "Curse", which summons {{Skeletron}}.

old-shaking-chest.name: Old Shaking Chest
old-shaking-chest.tooltip: The Old Shaking Chest is a rare entity that can be found in the {{Cavern}} layer after {{Skeletron}} has been defeated. It resembles a Chest and hops around like a slime. It requires a {{Golden Key}} to open.

plantera.name: Plantera
plantera.tooltip: Summon: {{@d Destroy}} {{Plantera's Bulb}} in the {{Underground Jungle}}

pumpking.name: Pumpking
pumpking.tooltip: The second and final miniboss in the {{Pumpkin Moon}} event.

queen-bee.name: Queen Bee
queen-bee.tooltip: Summon: Use {{Abeemination}} in the {{Jungle}} {{@s *OR*}} {{@d destroy}} {{larva}}, within {{Bee Hive (biome)}} in the {{Underground Jungle}}.

queen-slime.name: Queen Slime
queen-slime.tooltip: Summon: Use {{Gelatin Crystal}} found in the {{Underground Hallow}}

santa-nk1.name: Santa-NK1
santa-nk1.tooltip: A miniboss that spawns during the {{Frost Moon}} event.

skeletron.name: Skeletron
skeletron.tooltip: Summon: Speak to the {{Old Man}} at the {{Dungeon}} entrance at night {{@s *OR*}} {{@d kill}} {{Clothier}} with the {{Clothier Voodoo Doll}} at night

tax-collector.name: Tax Collector
tax-collector.tooltip: Spawn condition: Throw {{Purification Powder}} on a {{Tortured Soul}} (a rare Underworld enemy)

town-bunny.name: Town Bunny
town-bunny.tooltip: Arrives at dawn the day after the player uses the {{Bunny License}}, which is purchased from the {{Zoologist}} for 5 {{gold coin}}

town-cat.name: Town Cat
town-cat.tooltip: Arrives at dawn the day after the player uses the {{Cat License}}, which is purchased from the {{Zoologist}} for 5 {{gold coin}}

town-dog.name: Town Dog
town-dog.tooltip: Arrives at dawn the day after the player uses the {{Dog License}}, which is purchased from the {{Zoologist}} for 5 {{gold coin}}

wall-of-flesh.name: Wall of Flesh
wall-of-flesh.tooltip: Summon: Throw {{Guide Voodoo Doll}} into lava, while the guide is alive

windy-balloon.name: Windy Balloon
windy-balloon.tooltip:
The Windy Balloon is a unique flying enemy that only spawns during a {{Windy Day}} event at daytime.

 It can be seen carrying Slimes which are bound to the string underneath.

rubblemaker.name: Rubblemaker
rubblemaker.tooltip: Used with materials to place matching rubble. Right click to toggle placement size. Press Up/Down to cycle through styles. 'Not a piledriver nor a placeinator: It's a Rubblemaker'

solar-pillar.name: Solar Pillar
solar-pillar.tooltip: Drops {{Solar Fragment}} when defeated. The melee-themed {{Celestial Pillar}}, with minions that bear strong defensive abilities and deal heavy contact damage

nebula-pillar.name: Nebula Pillar
nebula-pillar.tooltip: Drops {{Nebula Fragment}} when defeated. The magic-themed {{Celestial Pillar}}, with minions capable of flight, teleportation, and other unusual attacks and abilities.

vortex-pillar.name: Vortex Pillar
vortex-pillar.tooltip: Drops {{Vortex Fragment}} when defeat. The ranged-themed {{Celestial Pillar}}, with fast, mobile minions capable of long-ranged projectile attacks.

stardust-pillar.name: Stardust Pillar
stardust-pillar.tooltip: Drops {{Stardust Fragment}} when defeat. The summoning-themed {{Celestial Pillar}}, with large swarms of enemies that spawn smaller, weaker entities to attack.

nerdy-slime.name: Nerdy Slime
nerdy-slime.tooltip: Will move in after {{@d killing}} {{King Slime}}.

cool-slime.name: Cool Slime
cool-slime.tooltip: Can move in when there is an active Party.

elder-slime.name: Elder Slime
elder-slime.tooltip: Moves in after unlocking an {{Old Shaking Chest}} in the {{Cavern}} Layer with a {{Golden Key}}.

clumsy-slime.name: Clumsy Slime
clumsy-slime.tooltip: Can be found floating with a balloon near floating islands in the {{Space}} Layer.

diva-slime.name: Diva Slime
diva-slime.tooltip: Moves in after throwing a {{Sparkle Slime Balloon}} into {{Shimmer}}.

surly-slime.name: Surly Slime
surly-slime.tooltip: Can be fished up during a {{Blood Moon}}.

mystic-slime.name: Mystic Slime
mystic-slime.tooltip: Moves in after using {{Purification Powder}} on a {{Mystic Frog}} in the Jungle.

squire-slime.name: Squire Slime
squire-slime.tooltip: Moves in after dropping a {{Copper Helmet}} or a {{Copper Shortsword}} on a Slime.

notoasis.name: notoasis
notoasis.tooltip: Only in Ocean Biomes, not in Deserts or the oasis

shimmer.name: Shimmer
shimmer.tooltip: Shimmer is a shiny, pearlescent liquid found in the {{Aether}}, a mini-biome which generates in the {{Underground}} or {{Cavern}} layer, always on the same side of the world as the {{Jungle}}.

alien-hornet.name: Alien Hornet
alien-hornet.tooltip:
The Alien Hornet is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Lunatic Cultist}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the area of the {{Vortex Pillar}} during the {{Lunar Events}}, summoned from opened portals in groups of two.

If not killed fast enough, it will fairly quickly grow into an {{Alien Queen}}.

They also grow from the {{Alien Larva}} that spawned from the death of an Alien Queen.

alien-larva.name: Alien Larva
alien-larva.tooltip:
The Alien Larva is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Lunatic Cultist}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the area of the {{Vortex Pillar}} during the {{Lunar Events}}.

They move fairly slowly along the ground, and will only naturally spawn in groups of two or three upon the death of an {{Alien Queen}}.

If unattended, they will grow into {{Alien Hornets}}, which in turn become Alien Queens later on.

alien-queen.name: Alien Queen
alien-queen.tooltip:
The Alien Queen is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Lunatic Cultist}} flying {{enemy}} that spawns in the area of the {{Vortex Pillar}} during the {{Lunar Events}}.

It attacks by shooting Alien Goop projectiles that have a 1/2 (50%) / {{||100%||}} chance to inflict the Distorted {{debuff}} for 6–12 / {{||5–9||}} seconds.

The debuff causes the player to randomly rise and descend in wave-like motions based on their horizontal position, practically preventing them from falling downwards.

It makes controlled descent impossible, but {{hooks}} and teleporting with the {{Rod of Discord}} can somewhat counteract this effect.

sleeping-angler.name: Sleeping Angler
sleeping-angler.tooltip:
The Angler is a unique {{NPC}} concerned exclusively with {{fishing}}.

He does not sell anything, but rather assigns {{quests}} to players.

Quest objectives generally involve catching him {{rare fish}} from particular {{biomes}} or {{layers}}, for which he offers various rewards.

angler-fish.name: Angler Fish
angler-fish.tooltip:
The Angler Fish is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} which spawns in the {{Cavern}} {{layer}} and in the {{Jungle}}.

It swims faster than {{Piranhas}} when chasing the player, and can inflict the {{Bleeding}} {{debuff}} for 45 / {{||90||}} / {{|||112.5|||}} seconds.

It has a small glowing bulb sprouting from its head that emits a small amount of light.

angry-bones.name: Angry Bones
angry-bones.tooltip:
The Angry Bones is an {{enemy}} found in the {{Dungeon}}.

They follow the {{Fighter AI}} and can open closed {{doors}} after one or two hits.

They will sometimes leap towards the player when they get close, or if the player is above them.

angry-dandelion.name: Angry Dandelion
angry-dandelion.tooltip:
The Angry Dandelion is an {{enemy}} that spawns on {{grass}} during the {{Windy Day}} {{event}} at daytime.

It is stationary and shoots 1–3 damaging Dandelion Seed projectiles at the player every 0.67 seconds.

angry-nimbus.name: Angry Nimbus
angry-nimbus.tooltip:
The Angry Nimbus is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns when the weather is {{Rainy}} in any {{Surface}} biome except for the {{Snow biome}}.

It will follow players around the map, attempting to kill them with a rain-type attack similar to that of the {{Crimson Rod}} and the {{Nimbus Rod}}.

It floats above the player, making it difficult to hit with close-range weapons.

angry-trapper.name: Angry Trapper
angry-trapper.tooltip: The Angry Trapper is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Jungle}} and {{Underground Jungle}} {{biomes}} and is a much stronger version of the {{Man Eater}} and {{Snatcher}}.

angry-tumbler.name: Angry Tumbler
angry-tumbler.tooltip:
The Angry Tumbler is an {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Desert}} during {{Sandstorms}}.

It attacks by rolling towards the player, moving at very high speeds when rolling with the flow of the {{Mighty Wind}}.

Combined with its considerable contact damage, this makes the Angry Tumbler difficult to deal with for new players.

anomura-fungus.name: Anomura Fungus
anomura-fungus.tooltip:
The Anomura Fungus is an {{enemy}} found in {{Glowing Mushroom biomes}} above the surface at any point in the game.

During {{Hardmode}}, they can also spawn in underground Glowing Mushroom biomes.

albino-antlion.name: Albino Antlion
albino-antlion.tooltip:
The Antlion is an {{enemy}} found in {{Deserts}} during the day and in {{Underground Deserts}} at any time.

It remains stationary with its head sticking out of the ground, turns its head towards the player when they are within its line of sight, and fires Sand Ball projectiles at them once every 3.33 seconds.

It can only launch projectiles at an upward angle (within about 45° to the left and right), so players that are approaching from lower ground are usually out of its line of fire.

Sand Balls turn into {{Sand Blocks}} upon hitting an object, and will gather on the ground, similarly to the projectiles fired by the {{Sandgun}}.

antlion.name: Antlion
antlion.tooltip:
The Antlion is an {{enemy}} found in {{Deserts}} during the day and in {{Underground Deserts}} at any time.

It remains stationary with its head sticking out of the ground, turns its head towards the player when they are within its line of sight, and fires Sand Ball projectiles at them once every 3.33 seconds.

It can only launch projectiles at an upward angle (within about 45° to the left and right), so players that are approaching from lower ground are usually out of its line of fire.

Sand Balls turn into {{Sand Blocks}} upon hitting an object, and will gather on the ground, similarly to the projectiles fired by the {{Sandgun}}.

antlion-charger.name: Antlion Charger
antlion-charger.tooltip:
The Antlion Charger is an {{enemy}} similar in appearance to the {{Antlion}}, and is found in the {{Underground Desert}} or on surface {{Deserts}} during {{pre-Hardmode}} {{Sandstorms}}.

Unlike the Antlion, the Antlion Charger will, as its name suggests, charge at the player instead of remaining stationary.

It can jump over blocks and will gradually build up speed while moving before charging the player.

It can rarely drop the {{Mandible Blade}}.

giant-antlion-charger.name: Giant Antlion Charger
giant-antlion-charger.tooltip:
The Antlion Charger is an {{enemy}} similar in appearance to the {{Antlion}}, and is found in the {{Underground Desert}} or on surface {{Deserts}} during {{pre-Hardmode}} {{Sandstorms}}.

Unlike the Antlion, the Antlion Charger will, as its name suggests, charge at the player instead of remaining stationary.

It can jump over blocks and will gradually build up speed while moving before charging the player.

It can rarely drop the {{Mandible Blade}}.

antlion-larva.name: Antlion Larva
antlion-larva.tooltip:
The Antlion Larva is an {{enemy}} that is only spawned when {{Antlion Eggs}} are destroyed.

Its speed, jump height, occurrence in numbers, and small size make it a significant threat in the early game.

antlion-swarmer.name: Antlion Swarmer
antlion-swarmer.tooltip:
The Antlion Swarmer is a flying {{enemy}} found in the {{Underground Desert}} or on surface {{Deserts}} during {{pre-Hardmode}} {{Sandstorms}}.

It is a large dragonfly-like creature that looks similar to the {{Antlion}} and {{Antlion Charger}}.

It should be treated with caution by newer players because of its health and {{knockback}} resistance.

giant-antlion-swarmer.name: Giant Antlion Swarmer
giant-antlion-swarmer.tooltip:
The Antlion Swarmer is a flying {{enemy}} found in the {{Underground Desert}} or on surface {{Deserts}} during {{pre-Hardmode}} {{Sandstorms}}.

It is a large dragonfly-like creature that looks similar to the {{Antlion}} and {{Antlion Charger}}.

It should be treated with caution by newer players because of its health and {{knockback}} resistance.

arapaima.name: Arapaima
arapaima.tooltip:
The Arapaima is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns in pools of {{water}} within the {{Jungle}} and {{Underground Jungle}} {{biomes}}.

It swims much faster than most fish and deals high {{damage}}, but it has no {{knockback}} resistance, so high-knockback weapons are useful.

The Arapaima can jump from the water and therefore is able to attack players on land close to the water.

It will not die naturally outside of the water, despite being a fish.

armored-skeleton.name: Armored Skeleton
armored-skeleton.tooltip:
The Armored Skeleton is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} which can be found in the {{Caverns}}.

It has a 1/6 (16.67%) chance to inflict the {{Broken Armor}} {{debuff}} for 2 / {{||4||}} / {{|||5|||}} minutes on contact.

armored-viking.name: Armored Viking
armored-viking.tooltip:
The Armored Viking is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} found in the {{Ice biome}}.

It is similar to the {{Undead Viking}}, but has greatly increased {{defense}}, damage and health.

baby-slime.name: Baby Slime
baby-slime.tooltip: The Baby Slime is a {{slime}} that is spawned in groups of 1–3 from a defeated {{Mother Slime}}.

basilisk.name: Basilisk
basilisk.tooltip:
The Basilisk is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} found in the {{Underground Desert}} {{biome}}.

The Basilisk attacks by charging quickly towards the player while being highly resistant to {{knockback}}.

It drops 1 to 3 {{Sturdy Fossils}} and has a rare chance to drop the {{Ancient Horn}}, which summons a {{mount}} version of the Basilisk.

black-recluse.name: Black Recluse
black-recluse.tooltip:
The Black Recluse is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns in and around {{Spider Nests}} in the {{Cavern}} {{layer}}.

It has a 1/10 (10%) chance to inflict the {{Acid Venom}} {{debuff}} for 4 / {{||8||}} / {{|||10|||}} seconds.

In {{Expert Mode}}, it gains a special spit attack that applies the {{Webbed}} debuff for 0.5–2.5 seconds to players.

In the {{Don't dig up}} and {{Get fixed boi}} {{secret world seeds}}, that spit attack can randomly place down {{Cobwebs}} in a 13×13 rectangular area centered on the location where projectiles disappear.

black-slime.name: Black Slime
black-slime.tooltip:
The Black Slime is a variety of {{slime}} that only spawns in the {{Cavern}} {{layer}}.

When a Black Slime attacks, it has a chance to inflict the {{Darkness}} {{debuff}}, which decreases the radius that light sources will illuminate, making it more difficult to see.

blood-crawler.name: Blood Crawler
blood-crawler.tooltip:
The Blood Crawler is an {{enemy}} commonly found in {{the Crimson}}.

In addition to walking, it can crawl on {{background walls}}.

blood-eel.name: Blood Eel
blood-eel.tooltip:
The Blood Eel is a flying {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that can be spawned by {{fishing}} in {{water}} during a {{Blood Moon}}.

When the bobber dips and the player attempts to reel in a fish, a Blood Eel or another enemy has a chance to spawn within a few tiles of the bobber in place of a fish.

Its behavior is rather similar to that of the {{Wyvern}}, though it moves considerably faster.

blood-feeder.name: Blood Feeder
blood-feeder.tooltip:
The Blood Feeder is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawn in pools of water in {{the Crimson}} during Hardmode.

They have higher stats than {{Corrupt}} and {{Vicious Goldfish}}.

blood-squid.name: Blood Squid
blood-squid.tooltip:
The Blood Squid is a flying {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} summoned by the {{Dreadnautilus}} during a {{Blood Moon}}.

It resembles a regular {{Squid}}, but is larger, redder-colored, and has multiple eyes on its body, similar to the {{Drippler}}.

The Dreadnautilus always summons Blood Squids in groups of three.

blood-zombie.name: Blood Zombie
blood-zombie.tooltip:
The Blood Zombie is a unique {{zombie}} variant that only appears during a {{Blood Moon}}.

It acts mostly the same as normal zombies, walking towards the player to deal contact damage while attempting to navigate around obstacles, and opening doors if they're in the way.

However, it speeds up considerably the closer it gets to the player, and it generally has greater health, damage, and speed than most other zombies.

It can rarely drop the {{Money Trough}}, the {{Shark Tooth Necklace}}, or the {{Bloody Tear}} when killed.

blue-armored-bones.name: Blue Armored Bones
blue-armored-bones.tooltip:
The Blue Armored Bones is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Dungeon}} after Plantera is defeated.

Similar to {{Angry Bones}}, they come in multiple varieties and follow the {{Fighter AI}}.

They also have a 1/2 (50%) chance to inflict the {{Broken Armor}} debuff for 10 / {{||20||}} / {{|||25|||}} seconds.

blue-slime.name: Blue Slime
blue-slime.tooltip:
The Blue Slime is a highly common variety of {{slime}}.

They appear in {{Forests}} during the {{day}}, or at any time in the {{Underground}} layer.

Blue Slimes will usually not target the player, but will become aggressive when damaged, during the {{night}}, or at any time when underground.

bone-lee.name: Bone Lee
bone-lee.tooltip:
Bone Lee is a rare {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Dungeon}} after Plantera is defeated.

It moves at an extraordinary speed, deals large amounts of damage, and has high {{knockback}} resistance.

It will also spawn at any time in the Dungeon regardless of the type of {{Dungeon wall}} present.

bone-serpent.name: Bone Serpent
bone-serpent.tooltip:
The Bone Serpent is a burrowing {{worm}}-like {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Underworld}}.

They consist of 14–20 segments in total.

brain-scrambler.name: Brain Scrambler
brain-scrambler.tooltip:
The Brain Scrambler is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Golem}} {{enemy}} that appears during the {{Martian Madness}} {{event}}.

It fires green laser projectiles, at a maximum fire rate of once every 0.67 seconds (40 ticks), which have a chance to inflict the {{Confused}} {{debuff}}.

brain-suckler.name: Brain Suckler
brain-suckler.tooltip:
The Brain Suckler is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Lunatic Cultist}} flying {{enemy}} that spawns in the area of the {{Nebula Pillar}} during the {{Lunar Events}}.

It latches onto the player's head, dealing large amounts of damage at a steady rate, while also inflicting the Obstructed {{debuff}} for the remainder of the time it is on the player.

The debuff causes the player's screen to darken significantly, except for a small area around the player, similar to the {{Blackout}} debuff.

It additionally has the effect of {{Bleeding}}, preventing all health regeneration.

butcher.name: Butcher
butcher.tooltip:
The Butcher is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} that only spawns during the {{Solar Eclipse}} {{event}}.

It slowly builds up speed over time, similarly to an {{Antlion Charger}}, and wields a bloodied chainsaw that will deal heavy damage to players on contact.

butterfly.name: Butterfly
butterfly.tooltip:
Butterflies are small {{critters}} that can be found in {{Forests}} during {{daytime}}.

They can be caught with a {{Bug Net}} (except for the Hell Butterfly) and carried in the inventory or crafted into {{Butterfly Jars}}.

They can also be used as {{bait}} for {{fishing}}.

The {{Underworld}} variant, Hell Butterfly, can be found in the Underworld during daytime.

cave-bat.name: Cave Bat
cave-bat.tooltip:
The Cave Bat is a common type of {{bat}} {{enemy}} found only in the {{Cavern}} layer.

It is fairly small and can be difficult to hit with certain weapons.

One is to proceed with caution when entering caves early on, as Cave Bats can pose a moderate threat when one is not prepared to deal with them.

In {{Expert Mode}}, they can inflict the {{Feral Bite}} {{debuff}}, making them more dangerous.

Like most other bats, they cannot enter {{water}}.

chaos-elemental.name: Chaos Elemental
chaos-elemental.tooltip:
The Chaos Elemental is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns on {{Pearlstone}}, {{Pearlsand}} and {{Pink Ice Blocks}} in the {{Cavern}} {{layer}}.

It runs around and teleports quickly, attempting to damage the player by making contact.

It will teleport anywhere in a random position within 20 tiles from its target, but at least 4 tiles away, and only into a space that is at least 4 tiles tall and 3 tiles wide.

It will not teleport into {{lava}}, though it can easily fall into it after teleporting.

spectral-elemental.name: Spectral Elemental
spectral-elemental.tooltip:
The Chaos Elemental is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns on {{Pearlstone}}, {{Pearlsand}} and {{Pink Ice Blocks}} in the {{Cavern}} {{layer}}.

It runs around and teleports quickly, attempting to damage the player by making contact.

It will teleport anywhere in a random position within 20 tiles from its target, but at least 4 tiles away, and only into a space that is at least 4 tiles tall and 3 tiles wide.

It will not teleport into {{lava}}, though it can easily fall into it after teleporting.

clinger.name: Clinger
clinger.tooltip:
The Clinger is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} found in the {{Underground Corruption}} {{biome}}.

Clingers spawn anchored to a {{block}}, with their head attached to an extendable "stem" that does not damage the player.

They attack by launching orbs of Cursed Flames at the player, which can inflict the {{Cursed Inferno}} {{debuff}}.

clown.name: Clown
clown.tooltip:
The Clown is an uncommon {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that only appears during {{Blood Moons}}.

Although it has a chance to inflict the {{Confused}} {{debuff}} upon contact with the player, its primary weapons are yellow Happy Bombs, which are thrown in an arc, bounce off surfaces, and deal high damage but do not destroy tiles.

Clowns can also throw {{Chattering Teeth Bombs}}.

clumsy-balloon-slime.name: Clumsy Balloon Slime
clumsy-balloon-slime.tooltip:
The Clumsy Balloon Slime is a rare entity that can be found in the {{Space}} layer.

It resembles a slime attached to a balloon, floating around midair.

It does not damage the player, nor does it take damage from any source.

The player can break its balloon with any weapons, tools, projectiles, etc., and then it will transform into the {{Clumsy Slime}}.

Afterwards, the Clumsy Balloon Slime will no longer spawn.

cochineal-beetle.name: Cochineal Beetle
cochineal-beetle.tooltip:
The Cochineal Beetle is a rare {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Cavern}} layer.

It drops the {{Red Husk}}, which can be used to make {{Red Dye}}.

corite.name: Corite
corite.tooltip:
The Corite is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Lunatic Cultist}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the area of the {{Solar Pillar}} during the {{Lunar Events}}.

It is a flying enemy that shrouds itself in fire before charging toward the player at high speeds, all while traveling through {{blocks}}.

corrupt-bunny.name: Corrupt Bunny
corrupt-bunny.tooltip: The Corrupt Bunny is an {{enemy}} that results from a {{Bunny}} that has been corrupted, either by the player using {{Vile Powder}} or by a {{Blood Moon}} event on a {{Corruption}} world.

corrupt-goldfish.name: Corrupt Goldfish
corrupt-goldfish.tooltip:
The Corrupt Goldfish is an {{enemy}} that results from a {{Goldfish}} being corrupted, either by the player using {{Vile Powder}} or by a {{Blood Moon}} event in a {{Corruption}} world.

Corrupt Goldfish can also spawn naturally in the waters of the {{The Corruption}}.

corrupt-penguin.name: Corrupt Penguin
corrupt-penguin.tooltip: The Corrupt Penguin is an enemy that results from a {{Penguin}} that has been corrupted, either by the player using {{Vile Powder}} or by a {{Blood Moon}} event on a {{Corruption}} world.

corrupt-slime.name: Corrupt Slime
corrupt-slime.tooltip:
The Corrupt Slime is a {{Hardmode}} {{slime}} appearing in {{The Corruption}} and {{Underground Corruption}}.

It splits into two to three {{Slimelings}} upon death.

Both its original form and the Slimelings can inflict the {{Darkness}} {{debuff}}.

shadow-slime.name: Shadow Slime
shadow-slime.tooltip:
The Corrupt Slime is a {{Hardmode}} {{slime}} appearing in {{The Corruption}} and {{Underground Corruption}}.

It splits into two to three {{Slimelings}} upon death.

Both its original form and the Slimelings can inflict the {{Darkness}} {{debuff}}.

corruptor.name: Corruptor
corruptor.tooltip:
The Corruptor is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns within {{The Corruption}} and resembles a larger version of the {{Eater of Souls}}.

It intermittently fires {{Vile Spit}} projectiles that inflict the {{Weak}} {{debuff}}.

crab.name: Crab
crab.tooltip:
The Crab is a weak {{enemy}} found in {{Oceans}}, walking along the bottom and occasionally jumping.

They may also walk on land, and whether or not they are in water has no effect on their behavior.

crawdad.name: Crawdad
crawdad.tooltip:
The Crawdad is an {{enemy}} found in the {{Cavern}} {{layer}}.

It has two color variants that share identical stats and behavior.

Crawdad acts like {{Crabs}}, walking along the ground and jumping at the player when in close enough proximity, though it is slightly faster.

It is also capable of moving through {{water}} unhindered.

When a Crawdad is right next to the player, it will attack with its claw, dealing high damage.

crawltipede.name: Crawltipede
crawltipede.tooltip:
The Crawltipede is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Lunatic Cultist}}, {{worm}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the area of the {{Solar Pillar}} during the {{Lunar Events}}.

Uniquely, it has no visible health bar, and its tail is the only segment that is vulnerable to attacks, which takes 10× damage.

It flies directly at airborne players and deals high damage while ignoring players on the ground.

The Crawltipede's primary role seems to be forcing the player to stay near the ground, opening them up to melee attacks from the other Solar Pillar enemies.

creature-from-the-deep.name: Creature from the Deep
creature-from-the-deep.tooltip:
The Creature from the Deep is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} from the {{Solar Eclipse}} {{event}}.

They attack like most other fighter-type enemies on land, but they also have the ability to swim.

While they are in water, their speed and maneuverability increases dramatically.

It is also capable of jumping out of the water towards the player, doing so with maneuverability similar to a {{Unicorn}}.

crimera.name: Crimera
crimera.tooltip:
The Crimera is a common flying {{enemy}} that spawns predominantly in {{The Crimson}}.

It is the Crimson counterpart to {{the Corruption}}'s {{Eater of Souls}}, and behaves in the same manner, attacking by flying toward the player while circling them at a distance before periodically rushing in.

crimslime.name: Crimslime
crimslime.tooltip:
The Crimslime is a {{Hardmode}} {{slime}} that spawns in {{the Crimson}}.

It spawns with either 85%, 100%, or 115% of its usual size, with the stats altered accordingly.

It has a chance to inflict the {{Darkness}} {{debuff}} on contact.

crimson-axe.name: Crimson Axe
crimson-axe.tooltip:
The Crimson Axe is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} which is found in the {{Underground Crimson}}.

It hovers and spins before lunging at the player, traveling through any {{blocks}} to reach them.

Any {{knockback}} stops its attack, forcing it to pause and spin up again.

It has a 1/3 (33.33%) chance to inflict the {{Cursed}} {{debuff}} for 4 / {{||8||}} / {{|||10|||}} seconds.

cultist-archer.name: Cultist Archer
cultist-archer.tooltip:
The Cultists are {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Golem}} {{enemies}} that spawn along with a {{Mysterious Tablet}} at the entrance to the {{Dungeon}} after Golem has been defeated.

Four Cultists will appear there: two Cultist Archers and two Lunatic Devotees.

Defeating all four of the Cultists causes the {{Lunatic Cultist}} {{boss}} to spawn instantly at their location, destroying the Mysterious Tablet in the process.

Defeating the Lunatic Cultist then initiates the {{Lunar Events}}.

lunatic-devotee.name: Lunatic Devotee
lunatic-devotee.tooltip:
The Cultists are {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Golem}} {{enemies}} that spawn along with a {{Mysterious Tablet}} at the entrance to the {{Dungeon}} after Golem has been defeated.

Four Cultists will appear there: two Cultist Archers and two Lunatic Devotees.

Defeating all four of the Cultists causes the {{Lunatic Cultist}} {{boss}} to spawn instantly at their location, destroying the Mysterious Tablet in the process.

Defeating the Lunatic Cultist then initiates the {{Lunar Events}}.

cursed-hammer.name: Cursed Hammer
cursed-hammer.tooltip:
The Cursed Hammer is a rare {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} which spawns in the {{Underground Corruption}}.

It hovers and spins before lunging at the player, traveling through any {{blocks}} to reach them.

Any {{knockback}} stops its attack, forcing it to pause and spin up again.

It can inflict the {{Cursed}} {{debuff}}.

shadow-hammer.name: Shadow Hammer
shadow-hammer.tooltip:
The Cursed Hammer is a rare {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} which spawns in the {{Underground Corruption}}.

It hovers and spins before lunging at the player, traveling through any {{blocks}} to reach them.

Any {{knockback}} stops its attack, forcing it to pause and spin up again.

It can inflict the {{Cursed}} {{debuff}}.

cursed-skull.name: Cursed Skull
cursed-skull.tooltip:
The Cursed Skull is a flying {{enemy}} found only in the {{Dungeon}}.

It is similar to the {{Meteor Head}}, with high damage, slow speed, and the ability to travel through tiles.

It floats slowly towards the player, occasionally stopping to float in a circle around them for a while, before suddenly lunging forward to attack, similar to the {{Eye of Cthulhu}}.

Upon striking a player, the Cursed Skull has a 1/3 (33.33%) chance of inflicting the {{Cursed}} {{debuff}} for 4 / {{||8||}} / {{|||10|||}} seconds.

This renders the player unable to use items, including {{weapons}}, {{potions}}, and {{hooks}}, making it very difficult to avoid taking further damage.

cyan-beetle.name: Cyan Beetle
cyan-beetle.tooltip:
The Cyan Beetle is a rare {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Ice biome}}.

When killed, it will always drop the {{Cyan Husk}}, which can be used to craft {{Cyan Dye}}.

dark-caster.name: Dark Caster
dark-caster.tooltip:
The Dark Caster is an {{enemy}} encountered in the {{Dungeon}}.

They follow the {{Caster AI}}, firing three {{Water Sphere}} projectiles before teleporting to another on-screen location.

Water Spheres travel through walls but can be destroyed with a single hit from any weapon.

deadly-sphere.name: Deadly Sphere
deadly-sphere.tooltip:
The Deadly Sphere is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Solar Eclipse}} {{event}}.

Upon spawning, it flies randomly in the direction of the player until it is within close enough proximity while traveling through {{blocks}}.

Upon noticing the player, the Deadly Sphere grows spikes and begins to spin before rapidly charging toward them indefinitely while attempting to remain directly on top of them.

While attacking the player, they are unable to charge through blocks.

demon.name: Demon
demon.tooltip:
The Demon is an {{enemy}} found in {{The Underworld}}.

It attacks by summoning volleys of four spinning purple blades that accelerate towards the player, which are similar those fired by the {{Demon Scythe}} spell that it has a chance to drop.

These blades have a chance to inflict {{Darkness}} and are stopped by any {{block}}, but cannot be destroyed with {{weapons}}.

demon-eye.name: Demon Eye
demon-eye.tooltip:
The Demon Eye is a common flying {{pre-Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that appears on most {{Surface}} biomes during the night.

It has a slow turn rate, follows the player, and bounces off blocks.

Its trajectory is changed when hit with a weapon that causes {{knockback}}.

derpling.name: Derpling
derpling.tooltip:
The Derpling is a {{Hardmode}}, insect-like {{enemy}} which spawns on the surface {{Jungle}} during the {{day}} in place of the {{Giant Flying Foxes}} which spawn at night.

It jumps high, can strafe to the sides in mid-air, and attempts to land on top of the player.

Derplings should be defeated quickly, as they spawn fast and can easily overwhelm the player.

desert-spirit.name: Desert Spirit
desert-spirit.tooltip:
The Desert Spirit is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that can be found in {{Corrupted}} or {{Crimson}} {{Underground Desert}} biomes.

The Desert Spirit has an attack pattern that is unique compared to most enemies.

Upon appearing near a player, it will cast a single Desert Spirit's Curse, a colored projectile that attempts to home in on the nearest player, phasing through {{blocks}} and slowly gaining speed.

After a certain amount of time, it will duplicate and explode.

The projectiles themselves do not deal any damage, but their explosions do.

Similar to other {{casters}}, the Desert Spirit will finally teleport to a different location and repeat the process.

diabolist.name: Diabolist
diabolist.tooltip:
The Diabolist is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Dungeon}} after Plantera is defeated.

It attacks by firing Inferno Bolts, blazing magic bolts that can travel through {{blocks}}, at the player.

These bolts explode, creating a large flaming sphere that lasts for 9 seconds and deals massive damage on contact.

Both the bolts and the spheres inflict the {{On Fire!}} {{debuff}} for 8–15 / {{||16–30||}} / {{|||20–37.5|||}} seconds.

Diabolists teleport to a different location on-screen after firing six projectiles or upon being hit.

digger.name: Digger
digger.tooltip:
The Digger is a worm-type {{enemy}} that appears in the {{Underground}} and {{Cavern}} {{layers}} in {{Hardmode}}.

On {{}}{{ Old-gen console}} and {{}}{{}}, it completely replaces the {{pre-Hardmode}} {{Giant Worm}}.

Giant Worms still spawn rarely in Hardmode, but share kill count and banner with Diggers.

doctor-bones.name: Doctor Bones
doctor-bones.tooltip:
Doctor Bones is a {{zombie}} variant that rarely appears in the {{Jungle}} and {{Underground Jungle}} at {{night}}.

It acts the same as normal zombies, slowly walking towards the player to deal contact damage, while attempting to navigate around obstacles and only being able to open doors during a {{Blood Moon}}.

It has significantly greater health than all other zombies and deals slightly higher damage.

It drops the {{Archaeologist's Hat}} when killed.

dr-man-fly.name: Dr. Man Fly
dr-man-fly.tooltip:
Dr.

Man Fly is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Solar Eclipse}} {{event}}.

He mainly attacks by throwing {{Toxic Flasks}} at the player, dealing area-of-effect damage.

He gives the player a warning about an oncoming Toxic Flask attack by pausing in place.

Like the {{Nailhead}} and {{Eyezor}}, they appear to traverse much slower than many of the other Solar Eclipse enemies, leaving the player enough time to plan a safe attack against them.

drakanian.name: Drakanian
drakanian.tooltip:
The Drakanian is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Lunatic Cultist}} ground-based {{enemy}} that spawns in the area of the {{Solar Pillar}} during the {{Lunar Events}}.

It can originally spawn riding a {{Drakomire}}, forming a {{Drakomire Rider}}, and each can be separately killed.

Killing the Drakomire first will cause the Drakanian to fully heal and attack the player.

In this case, the kill will not count towards the {{Drakomire Rider Banner}}.

There is no extra banner just for the Drakanians.

drakin.name: Drakin
drakin.tooltip:
The Drakin is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Old One's Army}} {{event}}.

It spawns during Tier 2 during waves 4–7, and Tier 3 during waves 3–7.

drakomire.name: Drakomire
drakomire.tooltip:
The Drakomire is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Lunatic Cultist}}, ground-based {{enemy}} that spawns in the area of the {{Solar Pillar}} during the {{Lunar Events}}.

It periodically comes to a stop and shoots three {{Solar Flares}} at the player.

{{Drakanians}} will occasionally spawn riding a Drakomire, resulting in a {{Drakomire Rider}}.

drakomire-rider.name: Drakomire Rider
drakomire-rider.tooltip: The Drakomire Rider is a combination of a {{Drakanian}} and a {{Drakomire}}, and spawns in the area of the {{Solar Pillar}} during the {{Lunar Events}}.

dreadnautilus.name: Dreadnautilus
dreadnautilus.tooltip:
The Dreadnautilus is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that can be spawned by {{fishing}} in {{water}} during a {{Blood Moon}}.

When the bobber dips and the player attempts to reel in a fish, a Dreadnautilus or another enemy ({{Wandering Eye Fish}}, {{Zombie Merman}}, {{Blood Eel}}, or {{Hemogoblin Shark}}) has a chance to spawn within a few tiles of the bobber in place of a fish.

The Dreadnautilus is rarer than the other four Blood Moon fishing enemies, spawning from only 1/10 (10%) of spawn attempts, on average.

It is widely considered the most difficult of these five enemies.

If the Dreadnautilus is not defeated by {{dawn}} (4:30 AM), it will fly away and despawn.

drippler.name: Drippler
drippler.tooltip:
The Drippler is a flying {{enemy}} that exclusively spawns during a {{Blood Moon}}.

It hovers above the ground and floats slowly towards the player, but can be easily {{knocked back}} with most {{weapons}}.

It can rarely drop the {{Shark Tooth Necklace}}, the {{Money Trough}}, or the {{Bloody Tear}}.

dune-splicer.name: Dune Splicer
dune-splicer.tooltip:
The Dune Splicer is a worm-type {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Underground Desert}}, or on the surface of the {{Desert}} during a {{Sandstorm}}.

It is the Hardmode counterpart of the {{Tomb Crawler}}.

dungeon-slime.name: Dungeon Slime
dungeon-slime.tooltip:
The Dungeon Slime is a {{slime}} that is found only in the {{Dungeon}}.

It is an indigo-colored {{slime}} that contains a {{Golden Key}} inside.

It always drops the Golden Key when defeated.

Unlike most other slimes, it does not drop {{Gel}}.

It can spawn anywhere in the Dungeon, but appears less frequently than other Dungeon {{enemies}}.

dungeon-spirit.name: Dungeon Spirit
dungeon-spirit.tooltip:
The Dungeon Spirit is a flying {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Dungeon}} after Plantera is defeated.

It can travel through {{blocks}} and drops {{Ectoplasm}}, which can be used to craft many {{Spectre}}-related {{items}}, as well as the {{Pumpkin Moon Medallion}} and {{Naughty Present}}.

eater-of-souls.name: Eater of Souls
eater-of-souls.tooltip:
The Eater of Souls is a flying {{enemy}} that spawns predominantly in {{The Corruption}}.

It is the Corruption counterpart to {{the Crimson}}'s {{Crimera}}.

Eaters of Souls spawn from both {{Ebonstone}} and {{Corrupt grass}}, with large groups coming from {{chasms}}.

They circle the player at a distance before periodically rushing in.

There are three size variants, each with corresponding amounts of health, defense, and damage.

elf-archer.name: Elf Archer
elf-archer.tooltip:
The Elf Archer is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} that starts to spawn beginning with wave 2 during the {{Frost Moon}} {{event}}.

It is very similar to the {{Pirate Crossbower}} and {{Skeleton Archer}}, shooting {{Flaming Arrows}} which inflict the {{On Fire!}} {{debuff}} for 7 / {{||14||}} / {{|||17.5|||}} seconds.

elf-copter.name: Elf Copter
elf-copter.tooltip:
The Elf Copter is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} that starts to spawn beginning with wave 6 during the {{Frost Moon}} {{event}}.

It travels through {{blocks}}, firing {{bullets}} rapidly at players within its line of sight.

etherian-goblin.name: Etherian Goblin
etherian-goblin.tooltip:
The Etherian Goblin is an {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Old One's Army}} {{event}}.

It is by far the event's most common enemy, spawning during every wave regardless of the current tier.

etherian-goblin-bomber.name: Etherian Goblin Bomber
etherian-goblin-bomber.tooltip:
The Etherian Goblin Bomber is an {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Old One's Army}} {{event}}.

It spawns during Tier 1 during waves 2–5, Tier 2 during waves 3 and 5–7, and Tier 3 during waves 1–2 and 5–7.

etherian-javelin-thrower.name: Etherian Javelin Thrower
etherian-javelin-thrower.tooltip:
The Etherian Javelin Thrower is an {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Old One's Army}} {{event}}.

It spawns during Tier 1 during waves 3–5, Tier 2 during waves 1–4 and 6–7, and Tier 3 during waves 1–4 and 6.

etherian-lightning-bug.name: Etherian Lightning Bug
etherian-lightning-bug.tooltip:
The Etherian Lightning Bug is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Golem}} {{enemy}} that spawns during {{Old One's Army}} {{event}}.

It spawns during Tier 3 i waves 2 and 6.

etherian-wyvern.name: Etherian Wyvern
etherian-wyvern.tooltip:
The Etherian Wyvern is a flying {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Old One's Army}} {{event}}.

It spawns during Tier 1 during waves 4–5, Tier 2 during waves 3–7, and Tier 3 during waves 3–4.

evolution-beast.name: Evolution Beast
evolution-beast.tooltip:
The Evolution Beast is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Lunatic Cultist}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the area of the {{Nebula Pillar}} during the {{Lunar Events}}.

It attacks by shooting a slow, homing Nebula sphere and attempting to ram the player.

It is advisable to keep Evolution Beasts away using {{weapons}} with high {{knockback}}, in order to avoid the powerful ram which is similar to that of a {{Unicorn}} or a {{Wolf}}.

eyezor.name: Eyezor
eyezor.tooltip:
The Eyezor is an uncommon {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that only spawns during a {{Solar Eclipse}}, and primarily attacks by advancing toward the player while firing high-velocity purple laser beams at them.

It travels faster than normal {{zombies}} and is highly resistant to {{knockback}}.

As the Eyezor takes damage, its movement speed and the rate at which it fires lasers increase exponentially.

face-monster.name: Face Monster
face-monster.tooltip:
The Face Monster is an {{enemy}} that can be found in {{the Crimson}}.

It follows the {{Fighter AI}}, being able to open {{doors}} at any time.

Like other Crimson enemies, it drops {{Vertebra}} fairly often.

fire-imp.name: Fire Imp
fire-imp.tooltip:
The Fire Imp is a {{caster}} enemy found only in the {{The Underworld}}.

It fires {{Burning Spheres}}, which travel through {{blocks}} and have a 1/3 (33.33%) chance to inflict the {{On Fire!}} {{debuff}}.

The Burning Spheres can easily be destroyed by hitting them with any {{weapon}}, {{minion}}, or damage-dealing {{tool}} or {{pet}}.

floaty-gross.name: Floaty Gross
floaty-gross.tooltip:
The Floaty Gross is a floating {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Underground Crimson}}.

It can float through {{blocks}} in a manner similar to {{Wraiths}} and has a 1/25 (4%) to inflict the {{Weak}} {{debuff}} for 2 / {{||4||}} / {{|||5|||}} minutes upon contact with the player.

Because of its low {{knockback}} resistance and ability to pass through walls, weapons with high knockback will be more effective against it in the narrow crevices of the Underground Crimson.

flocko.name: Flocko
flocko.tooltip:
The Flocko is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} that starts to spawn beginning with wave 11 of the {{Frost Moon}} {{event}}.

Flockos tend to spawn along with the {{Ice Queen}} and attack by flying toward the player while spinning at high speed.

flow-invader.name: Flow Invader
flow-invader.tooltip:
The Flow Invader is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Lunatic Cultist}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the area of the {{Stardust Pillar}} during the {{Lunar Events}}.

When it spawns, three smaller minions will appear one at a time and begin orbiting it.

The Flow Invader utilizes these as attacks, sending them as 1-2 high-speed projectiles towards the player.

The orbiting minions will reappear over time.

The Flow Invader will launch all of its remaining minions at the player upon death.

dutchman-cannon.name: Dutchman Cannon
dutchman-cannon.tooltip:
The Flying Dutchman is a {{Hardmode}} {{mini-boss}} that spawns during the {{Pirate Invasion}} {{event}}.

The Flying Dutchman emits golden particles while hovering above the player, and is armed with four cannons that fire explosive cannonballs toward the nearest player while occasionally spawning standard Pirate Invasion enemies that fall from the deck and onto solid ground.

The boss will be defeated and drop loot upon depleting the health of all four cannons.

flying-fish.name: Flying Fish
flying-fish.tooltip:
The Flying Fish is a flying {{enemy}} that spawns during {{rain}}.

It follows a path much like the {{Eater of Souls}}, but has relatively low movement speed.

Unlike most other flying enemies, it is able to freely enter water.

Flying Fish will ignore players not in their line of sight, e.g. behind a wall or under a roof.

flying-snake.name: Flying Snake
flying-snake.tooltip:
The Flying Snake is a {{flying enemy}} that spawns primarily in the {{Jungle Temple}}, alongside the {{Lihzahrd}}.

The only spawning requirement appears to be that the player stands in front of natural {{Lihzahrd Brick Walls}}.

frankenstein.name: Frankenstein
frankenstein.tooltip:
Frankenstein is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that only spawn during a {{Solar Eclipse}}.

During which, they are as common as {{Zombies}} are at night.

However, they hit much harder, have more life, and walk faster.

fritz.name: Fritz
fritz.tooltip:
Fritz is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} from the {{Solar Eclipse}} {{event}}.

It moves very quickly on the ground and leaps into players to attack.

frozen-zombie.name: Frozen Zombie
frozen-zombie.tooltip:
The Frozen Zombie is a {{Zombie}} variant that only appears on the {{Surface}} {{Snow biome}} at {{night}}.

It acts the same as normal Zombies, slowly walking towards the player to deal contact damage, while attempting to navigate around obstacles and only being able to open {{doors}} during a {{Blood Moon}}.

It is differentiated from other Zombies in that it is dressed in a light blue snow suit, which it can drop as {{armor}} pieces in the form of the {{Snow Hood}}, {{Snow Coat}}, and {{Snow Pants}}.

fungi-bulb.name: Fungi Bulb
fungi-bulb.tooltip:
The Fungi Bulb is an uncommon {{enemy}} found in {{Glowing Mushroom biomes}} above the surface.

During {{Hardmode}}, they can also appear in Underground Glowing Mushroom biomes.

The Fungi Bulb follows the plant behavior akin to the {{Man Eater}}, but is less aggressive.

Unlike its tougher {{Hardmode}} variant, the {{Giant Fungi Bulb}}, it does not fire spore projectiles.

gastropod.name: Gastropod
gastropod.tooltip: The Gastropod is a flying {{Hardmode}} {{slime}} that spawns frequently at the {{surface}} of {{The Hallow}} at {{night}}, and fires high-velocity Pink Lasers which emit faint pink light if the player is within its line of sight.

spectral-gastropod.name: Spectral Gastropod
spectral-gastropod.tooltip: The Gastropod is a flying {{Hardmode}} {{slime}} that spawns frequently at the {{surface}} of {{The Hallow}} at {{night}}, and fires high-velocity Pink Lasers which emit faint pink light if the player is within its line of sight.

ghost.name: Ghost
ghost.tooltip:
A Ghost is a character's representation upon {{death}} while playing in {{Hardcore}} mode.

Ghosts can continue to explore the world, fly limitlessly, and pass through blocks.

They cannot be seen by other players and cannot be harmed, nor can they attack, but can open doors, and they can continue to chat with other players in {{Multiplayer}}.

dreamer-ghoul.name: Dreamer Ghoul
dreamer-ghoul.tooltip:
Ghouls are {{Hardmode}} {{Underground Desert}} {{enemies}} who follow the {{Fighter AI}} (behaving similarly to {{Zombies}}).

Ordinary Ghouls spawn inside the normal Underground Desert, the Vile Ghoul inside {{Corrupt}} parts of the Underground Desert, the Tainted Ghoul inside {{Crimson}} parts of the Underground Desert, and the Dreamer Ghoul inside {{Hallowed}} parts of the Underground Desert.

ghoul.name: Ghoul
ghoul.tooltip:
Ghouls are {{Hardmode}} {{Underground Desert}} {{enemies}} who follow the {{Fighter AI}} (behaving similarly to {{Zombies}}).

Ordinary Ghouls spawn inside the normal Underground Desert, the Vile Ghoul inside {{Corrupt}} parts of the Underground Desert, the Tainted Ghoul inside {{Crimson}} parts of the Underground Desert, and the Dreamer Ghoul inside {{Hallowed}} parts of the Underground Desert.

tainted-ghoul.name: Tainted Ghoul
tainted-ghoul.tooltip:
Ghouls are {{Hardmode}} {{Underground Desert}} {{enemies}} who follow the {{Fighter AI}} (behaving similarly to {{Zombies}}).

Ordinary Ghouls spawn inside the normal Underground Desert, the Vile Ghoul inside {{Corrupt}} parts of the Underground Desert, the Tainted Ghoul inside {{Crimson}} parts of the Underground Desert, and the Dreamer Ghoul inside {{Hallowed}} parts of the Underground Desert.

vile-ghoul.name: Vile Ghoul
vile-ghoul.tooltip:
Ghouls are {{Hardmode}} {{Underground Desert}} {{enemies}} who follow the {{Fighter AI}} (behaving similarly to {{Zombies}}).

Ordinary Ghouls spawn inside the normal Underground Desert, the Vile Ghoul inside {{Corrupt}} parts of the Underground Desert, the Tainted Ghoul inside {{Crimson}} parts of the Underground Desert, and the Dreamer Ghoul inside {{Hallowed}} parts of the Underground Desert.

giant-bat.name: Giant Bat
giant-bat.tooltip:
The Giant Bat is a {{Hardmode}} {{bat}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Cavern}} {{layer}}.

It can inflict the {{Confused}} {{debuff}} for 5 / {{||10||}} / {{|||12.5|||}} seconds, causing the {{game controls}} to reverse.

It also has a 1/10 (10%) chance to inflict the {{Feral Bite}} debuff in {{Expert Mode}} for 30–90 seconds.

It appears much bigger than a normal {{Cave Bat}}.

giant-cursed-skull.name: Giant Cursed Skull
giant-cursed-skull.tooltip:
The Giant Cursed Skull is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Dungeon}} after Plantera is defeated.

It acts similarly to the {{Cursed Skull}} in flight pattern, except it is much larger and will occasionally shoot "Shadowflames", high-velocity white blasts that pass through tiles.

It has a chance to inflict the {{Cursed}} debuff upon contact, while its projectiles always inflict the debuff.

giant-flying-fox.name: Giant Flying Fox
giant-flying-fox.tooltip:
The Giant Flying Fox is a {{Hardmode}} {{bat}} {{enemy}} that spawns only on the surface of the {{Jungle}} at {{night}}, in addition to the common {{Jungle Bats}} and in place of the {{Derplings}} that spawn during the day.

In {{Expert Mode}}, it can inflict the {{Feral Bite}} {{debuff}}.

Giant Flying Foxes can deal significant damage to a player, and without any {{knockback}} resistance, can push players down Jungle caves.

They pose a significant threat to early Hardmode characters.

fungi-spore.name: Fungi Spore
fungi-spore.tooltip:
The Giant Fungi Bulb is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} found in {{Glowing Mushroom biomes}} both above and below the surface.

They act similar to {{Man Eaters}} and {{Snatchers}}, but have a longer reach and launch Fungi Spores towards the player, dealing high damage.

Fungi Spores fly towards their target, breaking upon contact with {{blocks}}, and glide downward in a serpentine pattern.

They can be destroyed by one hit with any {{weapon}} or damage-inflicting {{tool}}.

giant-shelly.name: Giant Shelly
giant-shelly.tooltip:
The Giant Shelly is an {{enemy}} found in the {{Cavern}} layer.

It has two color variants that share identical stats and behavior.

Giant Shelly walks around slowly, and occasionally curls into its shell.

It then starts spinning and hurls itself at the player, gaining increased defense and dealing high contact damage.

Giant Shellies attack very quickly and deal high damage compared with other enemies for the stage in the game where they are found.

giant-tortoise.name: Giant Tortoise
giant-tortoise.tooltip:
The Giant Tortoise is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Jungle}} and {{Underground Jungle}}.

It walks around slowly, and occasionally retracts into its shell.

It then starts spinning and hurls itself at the player, gaining increased defense and dealing high contact damage.

It drops {{Turtle Shells}}, which are vital for crafting {{Turtle armor}}.

giant-worm.name: Giant Worm
giant-worm.tooltip:
The Giant Worm is an {{Underground}} {{enemy}} that tunnels through {{blocks}} to attack the player, spawning in the {{Underground}} and {{Cavern}} layers.

It makes a distinctive shuffling sound that grows louder and more rapid as it approaches the player, providing an indicator of its presence and proximity.

It also leaves behind a subtle particle effect in its trail.

gigazapper.name: Gigazapper
gigazapper.tooltip:
The Gigazapper is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Golem}} {{enemy}} that appears during the {{Martian Madness}} {{event}}.

It uses a short-ranged electrical spear weapon unavailable to players, with a maximum fire rate of once every half of a second.

Its projectiles shock the player, inflicting the {{Electrified}} {{debuff}} for 4–8 seconds, causing the player to lose health rapidly.

gingerbread-man.name: Gingerbread Man
gingerbread-man.tooltip:
The Gingerbread Man is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Frost Moon}} {{event}}.

He is much like the {{Splinterling}} but drops no items.

gnome.name: Gnome
gnome.tooltip:
The Gnome is an {{enemy}}.

When it is not behind a {{background wall}} on the surface during the day, it will turn into a {{Garden Gnome}} which increases the {{luck}} of nearby players.

goblin-archer.name: Goblin Archer
goblin-archer.tooltip:
The Goblin Archer is an {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Goblin Army}} {{event}}.

It fires {{Wooden Arrows}} at players within its line of sight while stationary.

Its attacks can be interrupted by {{knocking it back}}.

goblin-peon.name: Goblin Peon
goblin-peon.tooltip:
The Goblin Peon is an {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Goblin Army}} {{event}}.

It is the event's most basic enemy and follows the {{Fighter AI}}.

Approximately one out of every five goblins will be a Peon (17.8% chance to spawn).

It has the unique ability to destroy {{doors}} that are in its way (destroyed doors drop as collectible items).

goblin-scout.name: Goblin Scout
goblin-scout.tooltip:
The Goblin Scout is an uncommon surface {{enemy}} found in the farthest edges of the player's world (only during the day).

It looks like an enemy from a {{Goblin Invasion}} event, but it spawns during ordinary gameplay.

The {{Tattered Cloth}} it drops is a component for the {{Goblin Battle Standard}}, which summons a Goblin Army.

goblin-sorcerer.name: Goblin Sorcerer
goblin-sorcerer.tooltip:
The Goblin Sorcerer is an {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Goblin Army}} {{event}}.

Approximately one out of every nine goblins will be a Sorcerer (11.11% chance to spawn).

The Goblin Sorcerer fires three {{Chaos Balls}} in succession that travel through {{blocks}} before it teleports.

The Sorcerer cannot fire and will not teleport when taking damage.

goblin-thief.name: Goblin Thief
goblin-thief.tooltip:
The Goblin Thief is an {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Goblin Army}} {{event}}.

It follows the typical {{Fighter AI}} but moves at a higher speed than similar enemies.

Approximately one out of every four goblins will be a Thief (23.7% chance to spawn).

bound-goblin.name: Bound Goblin
bound-goblin.tooltip: The Goblin Tinkerer is an {{NPC}} vendor who sells several unique items and also provides the {{reforge}} function.

goblin-warlock.name: Goblin Warlock
goblin-warlock.tooltip:
The Goblin Warlock is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns during a {{Goblin Invasion}} in Hardmode.

Because of her high stats and relatively high difficulty compared to other goblins, she will present a challenge for new players.

Defeating her, however, rewards the player with weapons that are themed after {{Shadowflame}}, all of which accordingly inflict the Shadowflame {{debuff}}.

goblin-warrior.name: Goblin Warrior
goblin-warrior.tooltip:
The Goblin Warrior is an {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Goblin Army}} {{event}}.

It follows the {{Fighter AI}} and is the most commonly encountered enemy in the event.

Approximately one out of every two goblins will be a Warrior (47.4% chance to spawn).

golem-head.name: Golem Head
golem-head.tooltip: Golem is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{boss}} found in the {{Jungle Temple}}.

granite-elemental.name: Granite Elemental
granite-elemental.tooltip:
The Granite Elemental is an {{enemy}} that spawns in {{Granite Caves}} and floats towards the player.

In {{Expert Mode}}, Granite Elementals will occasionally drop to the ground defensively when attacked, making them immune to all damage until they begin hovering again.

granite-golem.name: Granite Golem
granite-golem.tooltip:
The Granite Golem is an {{enemy}} found only in {{Granite Caves}}.

Every few seconds, it will shield itself from all attacks for two seconds, making it completely invulnerable.

It can still deal damage to players during this invulnerable state should they walk into it.

Like the {{Granite Elemental}}, it will always drop a small amount of {{Granite Blocks}}.

gray-grunt.name: Gray Grunt
gray-grunt.tooltip:
The Gray Grunt is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Golem}} {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Martian Madness}} {{event}}.

While outwardly similar to most Martian enemies, it is unique in that it is completely invulnerable to {{knockback}}.

It appears to have grey skin and black eyes.

green-slime.name: Green Slime
green-slime.tooltip:
The Green Slime is the weakest of all {{slimes}}, and is also one of the most common ones to encounter.

As Green Slimes spawn commonly in the {{Forest}} during the {{daytime}}, they are likely the first {{enemy}} a player will come across in a new world.

Green Slimes will not target the player under usual circumstances, but will become aggressive when damaged, during the night, or if brought underground.

harpy.name: Harpy
harpy.tooltip:
The Harpy is a flying {{enemy}} that spawns at high altitudes, typically around {{Floating Island}} heights.

Prior to {{Hardmode}}, they cannot spawn in the central vertical section of the map, which is around one-tenth the width of the entire map.

This restriction is removed in Hardmode.

They move following the {{Bat AI}}, and fire slow-moving blue feather projectiles in inaccurate bursts of three.

headless-horseman.name: Headless Horseman
headless-horseman.tooltip:
The Headless Horseman is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} that starts to spawn beginning with wave 5 of the {{Pumpkin Moon}} {{event}}.

He is an exceptionally dangerous enemy due to his speed, high contact damage, and immunity to {{knockback}}.

The Headless Horseman attacks by charging forward and doing a low, quick jump to reach the player.

Additionally, the Headless Horseman can also throw a Pumpkin Head projectile, which moves in a wavelike manner and homes into the player for an extended period of time.

hell-armored-bones.name: Hell Armored Bones
hell-armored-bones.tooltip:
The Hell Armored Bones is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} that spawn in the {{Dungeon}} after Plantera is defeated.

Similar to {{Angry Bones}}, they come in multiple varieties and follow the {{Fighter AI}}.

Hell Armored Bones are visible in darkness but do not actually emit light, except for a faint glow when immersed in {{water}}.

They also inflict the {{On Fire!}} debuff for 10 / {{||20||}} / {{|||25|||}} seconds.

hellbat.name: Hellbat
hellbat.tooltip:
The Hellbat is a {{pre-Hardmode}} {{bat}} {{enemy}} found only in {{The Underworld}}.

They are pesky creatures that can kill characters who are using early-game gear.

Though they cannot fly underwater or through {{lava}}, they take no damage from making contact with either.

hellhound.name: Hellhound
hellhound.tooltip:
The Hellhound is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} that starts to spawn beginning with wave 3 of the {{Pumpkin Moon}} {{event}}.

Hellhounds follow the {{Unicorn}} {{AI}} and will run quite fast at the player; the player will be barely able to outrun them with {{boots}} or similar movement accessories, thus attacking them while out of their reach is important while fighting them.

hemogoblin-shark.name: Hemogoblin Shark
hemogoblin-shark.tooltip:
The Hemogoblin Shark is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that can be spawned by {{fishing}} in {{water}} during a {{Blood Moon}}.

When the bobber dips and the player attempts to reel in a fish, a Hemogoblin Shark or another enemy has a chance to spawn within a few tiles of the bobber in place of a fish.

The Hemogoblin Shark can move extraordinarily fast on land, swim quickly in water, jump very high, and can fire a barrage of Blood Shot projectiles.

herpling.name: Herpling
herpling.tooltip:
The Herpling is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns in {{the Crimson}}.

It attacks the player by quickly jumping across the ground.

While it cannot jump very high, it can cover horizontal distance very quickly, making it a potent threat.

hoplite.name: Hoplite
hoplite.tooltip:
The Hoplite is an {{enemy}} that spawns in {{Marble Caves}}.

It throws piercing {{Javelins}} at the player, which can deal a lot of damage, especially if in {{Expert Mode}} or higher {{difficulties}}.

The Javelins they throw are also fairly accurate and are more likely to hit the player than most other enemy-fired projectiles.

This makes the Hoplite an enemy to avoid early on in the game until players are properly equipped and ready to face them.

hoppin-jack.name: Hoppin' Jack
hoppin-jack.tooltip:
The Hoppin' Jack is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that will only appear at {{night}} during the {{Halloween}} {{seasonal event}}, or in {{Graveyards}} at any time of year.

He attacks by hopping towards the player in a manner identical to most {{slimes}}.

The difference, however, is that the lower health he has, the quicker he becomes.

dragon-hornet.name: Dragon Hornet
dragon-hornet.tooltip:
The Hornet is a flying {{enemy}} found in the {{Underground Jungle}}.

They follow the {{Flying AI}}, floating just out of reach and then charging the player, while also firing stinger projectiles (at an increased rate in {{Expert Mode}}).

The stingers cannot be destroyed with weapons and travel at about the same speed as the darts from a {{Dart Trap}}.

Stingers have a 1/3 (33.33%) chance to inflict the {{Poisoned}} {{debuff}} on the player for 10 seconds.

In Expert Mode, the chance is 1/3 (33.33%) for 20 seconds and 2/3 (66.67%) for 2–10 seconds.

hornet.name: Hornet
hornet.tooltip:
The Hornet is a flying {{enemy}} found in the {{Underground Jungle}}.

They follow the {{Flying AI}}, floating just out of reach and then charging the player, while also firing stinger projectiles (at an increased rate in {{Expert Mode}}).

The stingers cannot be destroyed with weapons and travel at about the same speed as the darts from a {{Dart Trap}}.

Stingers have a 1/3 (33.33%) chance to inflict the {{Poisoned}} {{debuff}} on the player for 10 seconds.

In Expert Mode, the chance is 1/3 (33.33%) for 20 seconds and 2/3 (66.67%) for 2–10 seconds.

ice-bat.name: Ice Bat
ice-bat.tooltip:
The Ice Bat is a flying {{bat}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Ice biome}}.

Its icy nature causes struck players to be inflicted with the {{Chilled}} {{debuff}} and, more rarely, the {{Frozen}} debuff.

It emits a very faint glow, which can sometimes reveal nearby caverns.

ice-elemental.name: Ice Elemental
ice-elemental.tooltip:
The Ice Elemental is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that appears in the {{Snow biome}} at {{night}} and in the {{Ice biome}} at any time.

The Ice Elemental slowly hovers towards the player and shoots Frost Blasts which have a 1/3 (33.33%) chance to inflict the {{Frostburn}} {{debuff}} for 6 / {{||12||}} / {{|||15|||}} seconds.

ice-golem.name: Ice Golem
ice-golem.tooltip:
The Ice Golem is a rare {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Snow biome}} during {{Blizzards}} at any time of day or night.

The Ice Golem walks slowly, and, when it has a clear line of sight with the player, fires high-velocity Frost Beam projectiles that {{chill}} and occasionally {{freeze}} them in place.

Its movement speed and the rate at which it fires beams increase exponentially the further its health is depleted.

It has a guaranteed chance to drop a {{Frost Core}}, the main ingredient for the {{Frost armor}}, {{Cool Whip}}, and {{Blizzard in a Bottle}}.

ice-slime.name: Ice Slime
ice-slime.tooltip:
The Ice Slime is a type of {{slime}} that appears in the {{Snow biome}} during the daytime.

It may inflict the {{Chilled}} {{debuff}}, unless the player is already {{Frozen}}.

ice-tortoise.name: Ice Tortoise
ice-tortoise.tooltip:
The Ice Tortoise is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Ice biome}}.

It walks around slowly, and occasionally retracts into its shell.

It then starts spinning and hurls itself at the player, gaining increased defense and dealing high contact damage.

ichor-sticker.name: Ichor Sticker
ichor-sticker.tooltip:
The Ichor Sticker is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Underground Crimson}} and floats around in the air.

When tracking a player, it spits a stream of Ichor at them, similar to that of the {{Golden Shower}}.

The stream inflicts the {{Ichor debuff}} for 15 / {{||30||}} / {{|||37.5|||}} seconds, lowering the player's defense by 15.

Upon death, Ichor Stickers will also drop {{Ichor}}.

icy-merman.name: Icy Merman
icy-merman.tooltip:
The Icy Merman is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} found in the {{Ice biome}}.

Upon gaining line of sight of the player, it will stop in place and begin firing Icewater Spit projectiles similar to those fired by the {{Frost Staff}}, but with less frequency.

Its projectiles also behave similarly to those of the {{Ice Elemental}}, but deal less damage and are fired more rapidly.

The Icewater Spit attack always inflicts the {{Chilled}} {{debuff}}, and has a 1/10 (10%) chance to inflict {{Frozen}}.

illuminant-bat.name: Illuminant Bat
illuminant-bat.tooltip:
The Illuminant Bat is a {{Hardmode}} {{bat}} {{enemy}} that only spawns in the {{Underground Hallow}}.

It is the {{Hallowed}} version of the {{Giant Bat}}, but is much stronger and emits a blurred, after-image effect.

illuminant-slime.name: Illuminant Slime
illuminant-slime.tooltip:
